words.def: very much
words.example: People's reaction to the film has varied greatly.

words.def: a shop that sells food and other things used in the home. In American English ‘grocery store’ is often used to mean ‘supermarket’.
words.example: the corner grocery store

words.def: a formal promise that you will do something or that something will happen
words.example: They are demanding certain guarantees before they sign the treaty.

words.def: a set of rules or instructions that are given by an official organization telling you how to do something, especially something difficult
words.example: The government has drawn up guidelines for schools during the pandemic.

words.def: the place where a particular type of animal or plant is normally found
words.example: The panda's natural habitat is the bamboo forest.

words.def: the part of a door, window, etc. that you use to open it
words.example: She turned the handle and opened the door.

words.def: to attach something, or to be attached, at the top so that the lower part is free or loose
words.example: hang something + adv./prep., Hang your coat on the hook.

words.def: an area of water on the coast, protected from the open sea by strong walls, where ships can shelter
words.example: Several boats lay at anchor in the harbour.

words.def: damage or injury that is caused by a person or an event
words.example: He would never frighten anyone or cause them any harm.

words.def: causing damage or injury to somebody/something, especially to a person’s health or to the environment
words.example: the harmful effects of alcohol

words.def: a place from which an organization or a military operation is controlled; the people who work there
words.example: The firm’s headquarters is/are in London.

words.def: to become healthy again; to make something healthy again
words.example: It took a long time for the wounds to heal.

words.def: the service of providing medical care
words.example: the costs of healthcare for the elderly

words.def: the ability to hear
words.example: Her hearing is poor.

words.def: the place believed to be the home of God where good people go when they die, sometimes imagined to be in the sky
words.example: the kingdom of heaven

words.def: the back part of the foot below the ankle
words.example: Apply the cream to dry, cracked heels before bed.

words.def: an aircraft without wings that has large blades (= long flat parts) on top that go round. It can fly straight up from the ground and can also stay in one position in the air.
words.example: a police/rescue helicopter

words.def: the place believed to be the home of devils and where bad people go after death
words.example: He was terrified of going to hell when he died.

words.def: a type of hard hat that protects the head, worn, for example, by a police officer, a soldier or a person riding a bike or motorbike or playing some sports
words.example: Police in riot helmets lined the streets.

words.def: for this reason
words.example: We suspect they are trying to hide something, hence the need for an independent inquiry.

words.def: a plant whose leaves, flowers or seeds are used to add taste to food, in medicines or for their pleasant smell. parsley, mint and oregano are all herbs.
words.example: a herb garden

words.def: to be slow to speak or act because you feel uncertain or nervous
words.example: She hesitated before replying.

words.def: something that is hidden is kept or located in a place where it cannot be seen
words.example: Hidden dangers lurk in the ocean depths.

words.def: measuring a long distance from the bottom to the top
words.example: What's the highest mountain in the US?

words.def: the best, most interesting or most exciting part of something
words.example: One of the highlights of the trip was seeing the Taj Mahal.

words.def: a main road for travelling long distances, especially one connecting and going through cities and towns
words.example: Plans were being made for the construction of a new interstate highway system.

words.def: extremely funny
words.example: a hilarious joke/story

words.def: the area at either side of the body between the top of the leg and the middle part of the body; the joint at the top of the leg that connects it with the top part of the body
words.example: She stood with her hands on her hips.

words.def: the act of paying to use something for a short time
words.example: a hire car

words.def: a person who studies or writes about history; an expert in history
words.example: No serious historian today accepts this theory.

words.def: the action of holding somebody/something; the way you are holding somebody/something
words.example: hold on somebody/something, His hold on her arm tightened.

words.def: having a hole or empty space inside
words.example: a hollow ball/centre/tube/tree

words.def: connected with God or a particular religion
words.example: the Holy Bible

words.def: having no home, and therefore typically living on the streets
words.example: The scheme has been set up to help homeless people.

words.def: the quality of being honest
words.example: She answered all my questions with her usual honesty.

words.def: great respect for somebody
words.example: the guest of honour (= the most important one)

words.def: used to express what you hope will happen
words.example: Hopefully, we'll arrive before dark.

words.def: a person who invites guests to a meal, a party, etc. or who has people staying at their house
words.example: Ian, our host, introduced us to the other guests.

words.def: a building for people to live in, usually for one family
words.example: in a house, We live in a two-bedroom house.

words.def: all the people living together in a house or flat
words.example: Most households now own at least one car.

words.def: houses, flats, etc. that people live in, especially when referring to their type, price or condition
words.example: rental/student housing

words.def: funny; showing a sense of humour
words.example: He gave a humorous account of their trip to Spain.

words.def: the quality in something that makes it funny; the ability to laugh at things that are funny
words.example: It was a story full of gentle humour.

words.def: the state of not having enough food to eat, especially when this causes illness or death
words.example: Around fifty people die of hunger every day in the camp.

words.def: an act of looking for somebody/something that is difficult to find
words.example: The hunt is on for a suitable candidate.

words.def: going after and killing wild animals as a sport or for food
words.example: He goes hunting every weekend.

words.def: injured physically
words.example: None of the passengers were badly hurt.

words.def: an idea or explanation of something that is based on a few known facts but that has not yet been proved to be true or correct
words.example: to formulate/confirm a hypothesis

words.def: a small symbol on a computer or smartphone screen that represents a program or a file
words.example: Click on the printer icon with the mouse.
